core_payload.content:
# Symphony Workflow Safety

## 1. Role
Symphony Workflow Safety owns the repo-law and guardrail surfaces that constrain orchestration. It keeps workflow contract, approval and sandbox posture, night-shift gating, and the testing or security boundaries together so execution remains reviewable and safe under automation pressure.

## 2. Workflow Safety Surface
This sub-hub groups `symphony-workflow`, `symphony-approval-sandbox-policy`, `night-shift-autonomy`, `testing`, and `security`. Together they define how the orchestrator loads repository law, enforces execution posture, constrains autonomous night work, and stays accountable to verification and defensive controls.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own assignment discovery, daemon continuity, or attempt and retry evidence. Those responsibilities belong to `assignment-intake`, `live-session-fabric`, and `run-observability`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Workflow Safety groups the repo-law and guardrail surfaces of the orchestration system: Symphony Workflow, approval and sandbox policy, Night Shift Autonomy, Testing, and Security. It keeps execution contract, safety posture, autonomous-night gating, verification discipline, and defensive controls together so automation stays reviewable under pressure. This boundary matters because orchestrated coding work needs one explicit place where repo-owned law and safety constraints remain visible instead of being diffused across daemon logic or retry loops. It gives Symphony a clear governance seam before execution scales further.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Symphony decomposition to isolate repo workflow law, safety posture, night gating, and verification guardrails from live runtime continuity.
